sanskrit:
आयेऽभिजनवानस्मि कोऽन्योऽस्ति सदृशो मया।
यक्ष्ये दास्यामि मोदिष्य इत्यज्ञानविमोहिताः॥
transliteration: āḍhyo ’bhijanavān asmi ko ’nyo ’sti sadṛśo mayā | yakṣye dāsyāmi modiṣya ity ajñāna-vimohitāḥ ||
literal_meaning: I am wealthy and of high birth; who else is equal to me? I shall sacrifice, give charity, and rejoice—thus deluded by ignorance.
contextual_meaning: The demoniac mind culminates in grandiose self-appraisal and performative virtue, masking inner emptiness with external displays of power and generosity.
psychological_state: Narcissistic personality fortress: inflated self-image compensates for core shame, uses charity and ritual as reputation management.
trigger_scenario: Tech-bro tweets: ‘Built a unicorn before 30, donating 1 % to look good. Who’s matching me?’ while secretly dreading impostor feelings.
anchor_text: True worth is measured by humility, not wealth, status, or showy acts.
